title: Marine mammal sex determination using epidermal tissue from suction-cup tags
author: Sadie Novak

steps.title: Retrieve the tag from the water
steps.instruction: Retrieve the tag from the water. While retrieving the tag, ensure that nothing touches the suction cups with the exception of the net used for retrieval and wear gloves to prevent contamination.

steps.title: Using fresh nitrile gloves, wipe the...
steps.instruction: Using fresh nitrile gloves, wipe the inside of the suction cups with a sterile swab, avoiding the outside of the suction cup and any other part of the tag. This reduces the chances of obtaining a sample that is contaminated with the genetic material of another animal that may have come into contact with the tagged animal. Note that suctions cups may not have visible skin.

steps.title: Place the swab tip first into...
steps.instruction: Place the swab tip first into a plastic vial, trimming the handle as needed in order to close or screw on the lid.

steps.title: Clearly label the vial with the...
steps.instruction: Clearly label the vial with the date and the identifying information that is associated with that specific deployment. Make sure that there is enough information on the label to ensure that all samples are easily and accurately associated with the respective deployment data. This includes, at a minimum, the ID associated with the tag deployment. For example 'bb22_115a', indicating the two letter species ID (bb), julian day (115), and the order of deployment (a, the first tag of the day).

steps.title: Repeat steps 3 through 6 for...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 3 through 6 for the remaining pair of suction cups.

steps.title: Move the vial immediately into a...
steps.instruction: Move the vial immediately into a -80 °C freezer if possible. If no -80 °C is available on board the vessel, store on dry ice until the sample can be moved into an appropriate freezer.

steps.title: It is also important to note...
steps.instruction: It is also important to note this information on a datasheet designed to store this data along with other metadata that will be needed later. Of particular importance are: Date the sample was collected Time the sample was collected Location (latitude and longitude) the sample was collected Group size/proximity to other animals, as these factors may be a source of contamination

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW2...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW2 to each sample as directed by the manufacturer's protocol and centrifuge samples at 17000 x g, , for 00:03:00 to completely dry the column membrane. Discard the resulting flowthrough and collection tube. Place spin column in a clean 2 mL collection tube (included in kit). This step is important to ensure no residual ethanol is carried in downstream processing steps of the DNA extraction. If the bottom of the spin column comes in contact with any flowthrough solution, place spin column in a clean 2 mL collection tube and repeat the centrifugation step ( 17000 x g, , for 00:03:00 )

steps.title: Prepare the following reaction mixture for...
steps.instruction: Prepare the following reaction mixture for each sample of interest in 0.2 mL PCR-grade tubes: %s %s DNA Template 2 µLForward SRY Primer (20µM).5 µLReverse SRY Primer (20µM).5 µLForward ZFX/ZFY Primer (20µM).5 µLReverse ZFX/ZFY Primer (20µM).5 µLMyTaq HS Red Mix, 2x12.5 µLWater (dH2O)8.5 µL If necessary (to increase band intensity during analysis by gel electrophoresis), the volume of template DNA added to the reaction can be increased. To keep the final volume of the reaction at 25 µL , offset the added volume from the template DNA by subtracting the same volume of deionized water when calculating the reaction components before mixing.

steps.title: Amplify DNA fragments of interest with...
steps.instruction: Amplify DNA fragments of interest with 30 cycles of PCR at the following conditions: %s %s StepTemperatureTimeCyclesInitial Denaturation95 ℃3 min1Denaturation95 ℃15 sec35 Annealing55 ℃15 secExtension72 ℃20 sec

steps.title: Cast a 2 % volume agarose...
steps.instruction: Cast a 2 % volume agarose gel dyed with SYBR Safe DNA gel stain.

steps.title: DNA extraction was performed with a...
steps.instruction: DNA extraction was performed with a commercially available kit (DNease Blood & Tissue Kit, Qiagen, cat. no. 69504). To begin DNA extraction, remove swab tip from plastic stick. This can be done by tracing a clean razor blade along the connection point from the swab tip to the swab stick. Transfer the swab tip to a clean 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube for each sample. Be sure to use a clean razor blade for each sample to avoid cross-contamination of any genetic material.

steps.title: The PCR product will be assessed...
steps.instruction: The PCR product will be assessed by gel electrophoresis using the known length of the SRY (332 bpd), ZFX (245), and ZFY (212). This protocol is written for use with an EasyCast B1 Mini Gel Electrophoresis system (ThermoFisher).

steps.title: Swab two suction cups with one...
steps.instruction: Swab two suction cups with one swab, then the other two suction cups with a new sterile swab. Retrieving two samples from the same tag allows for more testing as well as sample security in cases of contamination.

steps.title: Re-position gel tray with gel in...
steps.instruction: Re-position gel tray with gel in the buffer chamber of the gel electrophoresis apparatus to the running position (wells should be placed in the chamber so that they are positioned at the negative electrode). Fill the buffer chamber with 1X TAE buffer so that the gel is completely submerged. Remove comb.

steps.title: Load 2
steps.instruction: Load 2.5 µL of 100 bp DNA molecular weight marker (New England Biolabs, cat. no. NEB#B7025) prepared to contain 1X bromophenol blue running dye into the first well (bromophenol blue runs at ~500 bp). Load 10 µL of the sample(s) in the following wells.

steps.title: Place the lid on the electrophoresis...
steps.instruction: Place the lid on the electrophoresis apparatus and ensure that cables are connected to the power supply. Run the gel at 140 V under dark conditions. Monitor gel run until the bromophenol blue running dye line has migrated about 1 cm from the midpoint of the gel (~ 00:35:00 - 00:45:00 ).

steps.title: Once the gel run is complete,...
steps.instruction: Once the gel run is complete, resolved DNA fragments may be visualized with a UV transilluminator. In this protocol, a ChemiDoc imaging system (Bio-Rad) was used to image the resolved PCR products following separation by gel electrophoresis. The blue tray and the protocol pre-programmed in the instrument for use with SYBR Safe DNA stain was used.

steps.title: The expected molecular weight of the...
steps.instruction: The expected molecular weight of the PCR product associated with the SRY gene is 332 bp and the expected molecular weight of the PCR product associated with the ZFX and ZFY genes are 245 and 212 bp, respectively. The presence of the SRY gene suggests that the DNA sample came from a male individual. Females will not possess this gene, and thus this DNA fragment will not be amplified in DNA extracted from a female. However, absence of this gene may be caused by issues in the experimental workflow, such as an insufficient amount of template DNA added, which would result in a false result. To validate the PCR reaction and minimize the chance a false sexing, DNA is also screened simultaneously for a gene that is present in both male and female individuals. The ZFX gene serves as a positive control in this protocol, and the ZFY gene serves as an additional indicator for males. An expected profile for DNA extracted from a male individual would show the presence of DNA fragments from both the SRY gene (332 bp) and the ZFX and ZFY genes (245 and 212 bp). An expected profile for DNA extracted from a female individual would show presence of only the DNA fragement from the ZFX gene (245 bp). A sample that showed presence of only the DNA fragment from the SRY gene would suggest non-specific priming and would be considered an inconclusive result. Figure 1: The results of three samples of extracted DNA, amplified following the protocol described above, and resolved via gel electrophoresis. Samples run in lanes 1 and 2 show an amplicon profile indicative of a male individual, with three bands at 332 bp (solid purple arrow), 245 bp (dashed blue arrow), and 212 bp (dotted green arrow). The sample run in lane 3 also displayed the amplicon profile of a male but only displayed a band at 332 bp (solid purple arrow). Samples with an amplicon profile indicative of a female individual showed one band resolved at 245 bp (dashed blue arrow), only.

steps.title: PCR amplification was performed with OneTaq...
steps.instruction: PCR amplification was performed with OneTaq DNA polymerase (New England BioLabs, cat. no. M0480S) and the standard reaction buffer supplied with the enzyme. PCR amplification was performed on extracted DNA samples using primers specific to the SRY (sex-determining region Y) and ZFX/ZFY (sex chromosomal zing-finger) genes. Oligonucleotide primers for both of these genes have been validated in other species (Bérubé et al., 1996; Suárez-Menéndez et al., 2024). Primers were ordered from IDT DNA. Forward SRY: 5' TGT GAA CGG TGA GGA TTA 3' Reverse SRY: 5' GTG CAT GGC TCG TAG TCT 3' Forward ZFX: 5’ ATA GGT CTG CAG ACT CTT CTA 3’ Reverse ZFX: 5’ CAC TTA TGG GGG TAG TCC TTT 3’ Primers updated 5/29/2025. These primers yield better results for baleen whales compared to Version 1. This also provides two confirmations of males (SRY and ZFY). PCR conditions and bpd are adjusted accordingly. Martine Bérubé and Per Jakob Palsbøll. Marcos Suárez-Menéndez, Martine Bérubé, Lutz Bachmann, Peter Best, Nick Davison, Mads Peter Heide-Jørgensen, Véronique Lesage, Tom Oosting, Rui Prieto, Christian Ramp, Jooke Robbins, Richard Sears, Mónica A. Silva, Mariel T.I. ten Doeschate, Marc Tollis, Els Vermeulen, Gísli A. Víkingsson, Øystein Wiig, Per J. Palsbøll.

steps.title: Add 180 µL of Buffer ATL...
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL of Buffer ATL directly to each tube containing swab as directed by the manufacturer's protocol. Pipette solution up and down and over entirety of swab thoroughly, at least 20 times.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K to each tube containing the swab and previous buffer solution as directed by the manufacturer's protocol. Pipette the solution up and down over the swab to mix. Vortex each sample for 00:00:15 on high to ensure thorough mixing.

steps.title: Incubate samples at 56 °C in...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at 56 °C in a hot water bath for 03:00:00 .

steps.title: Add 200 µL of Buffer AL...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of Buffer AL to each sample as directed by the manufacturer's protocol and mix thoroughly by quickly vortexing for 00:00:15 . Immediately add 200 µL of 96 % (v/v) EtOH and mix again by vortex for 00:00:15 .

steps.title: Pipette solution into the DNeasy Mini...
steps.instruction: Pipette solution into the DNeasy Mini spin column and place column in a 2 mL collected tube (included in kit). Incubate samples at Room temperature for 00:05:00 before centrifuging samples at 6000 x g, , for 00:05:00 . Discard flowthrough and collection tube. Place spin column in a clean 2 mL collection tube (included in kit). Do not discard flowthrough in this step or any step in the downstream processing into bleach. Chloroform gas will be generated.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW1...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW1 to each sample as directed by the manufacturer's protocol and centrifuge samples at 6000 x g, , for 00:05:00 . Discard the resulting flowthrough and collection tube. Place the spin column in a clean 2 mL collection tube (included in kit).

steps.title: Place the dried spin column into a clean 1
steps.instruction: Place the dried spin column into a clean 1.5 mL eppendorf tube and apply 100 µL Buffer AE onto the center of the column membrane. Be sure not to touch the membrane with the pipette tip. Allow the buffer to incubate on the membrane for 00:05:00 at room temperature. Elute DNA by centrifuging the column at 6000 x g, , for 00:01:00 . Elute DNA from the column again by applying 100 µL Buffer AE onto the center of the column membrane and centrifuging at 6000 x g, , for 00:01:00 . The elution step can be performed once with 200 µL of buffer. Eluting DNA step-wise will provide an increased DNA yield. Do not exceed an elution volume of 200 µL to ensure no flowthrough is refluxed into the bottom of the spin column. This may yield samples with lower purity.

steps.title: Dry DNA samples in a speed...
steps.instruction: Dry DNA samples in a speed vac overnight at room temperature. Re-solubilize DNA in 20 µL of ultrapure water. Measure sample concentration (ng/uL) and purity (260/280 value) using a Nanodrop.

steps.title: Dilute running dye in PCR product...
steps.instruction: Dilute running dye in PCR product mixture to a final concentration of 1X xylene cyanol (runs at ~4000 kbp) by adding 4 µL of a 6X xylene cyanol solution to each sample product.

steps.title: Prepare mold by placing gel tray...
steps.instruction: Prepare mold by placing gel tray in the buffer chamber of the electrophoresis apparatus so that the gaskets of the tray form a seal with the walls of the chamber. Use a level to adjust the tray position so that the gel is cast evenly.

steps.title: Prepare the 2 % volume agarose...
steps.instruction: Prepare the 2 % volume agarose solution by combining the appropriate mass of agarose with the appropriate volume of 1X tris-acetate EDTA (TAE) buffer (i.e. for a 75 mL agarose gel, combine 1.875 g agarose in 75 mL 1X TAE buffer).

steps.title: Melt agarose by microwaving solution in...
steps.instruction: Melt agarose by microwaving solution in 00:00:15 - 00:00:30 time increments, swirling flask between heatings.

steps.title: Allow solution to cool at room...
steps.instruction: Allow solution to cool at room temperature for 00:05:00 before adding appropriate volume of SYBR Safe dye to the solution to dilute to 1X SYBR Safe DNA gel stain (i.e. for a 75 mL agarose gel, add 7.5 µL 10,000X SYBR Safe DNA gel stain to solution). If solution polymerizes in its melting vessel before it is stained and cast, the solid agarose solution can be re-melted as described in step 21.3. If the solid agar was stained and polymerized before casting, the gel can be melted, but should be re-stained to a final concentration of 1X stain. Other DNA stains such as GelRed or Ethidium Bromide (EtBr) may be used for this step, at appropriate concentrations. SYBRSafe is recommending for its biosafety properties and resulting ease of use.

steps.title: Once agarose solution is stained, cast...
steps.instruction: Once agarose solution is stained, cast immediately in appropriate gel mold. Place a comb in the mold to create a row of wells. Allow gel to polymerize at Room temperature for 00:20:00 under dark conditions to protect the DNA stain.
